sourceMovie.title: The Devil Wears Prada
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Devil Wears Prada (2006) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Proposal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Devil Wears Prada clicked for you, The Proposal (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Devil Wears Prada?
faq.answer: Try The Proposal, Morning Glory, Never Been Kissed—each shares DNA with The Devil Wears Prada while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Devil Wears Prada?
faq.answer: The Devil Wears Prada (2006) is best described as Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Devil Wears Prada part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Devil Wears Prada Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
